subject: english
learning_objectives: Identify common prefixes and the words they form
Identify common suffixes and the words they form
Use prefixes and suffixes to expand vocabulary

sections.heading: Prefixes – Definition and Examples
sections.content:
A prefix is a word or syllable placed before a root word to form a new word.

un-   + happy → unhappy | un- + faithful → unfaithful
dis-  + appear → disappear | dis- + respect → disrespect
non-  + sense → nonsense | non- + existence → non-existence
mis-  + lead → mislead | mis- + direct → misdirect
mal-  + treat → maltreat | mal- + practice → malpractice
out-  + wit → outwit | out- + run → outrun
trans- + plant → transplant
inter- + denominational → interdenominational | inter- + state → interstate
post- + war → post-war | post- + independence → post-independence

sections.heading: Suffixes – Definition and Examples
sections.content:
A suffix is a word or syllable placed after a root word to form a new word.

-ness  + happy → happiness
-ment  + punish → punishment
-ship  + apprentice → apprenticeship | -ship + relation → relationship
-ation + found → foundation | -ation + associate → association
-ant   + inhabit → inhabitant | -ant + serve → servant
-hood  + boy → boyhood | -hood + child → childhood
-less  + child → childless | -less + guilt → guiltless
